test_cases.claim: Founded in 1984 by business executives, Cortec Group is a New York–based private equity firm
Cortec has raised eight institutional funds
Cortec Group is a New York–based private equity firm that positions itself as distinct from most peers, citing a heritage rooted in business and operations rather than Wall Street
The firm reports achieving top-quartile-or-better returns across its three fully realized funds.
It is currently investing Cortec Group Fund VIII, which closed at approximately $3.2 billion in February 2024.
Total transaction value: ~$14 billion
Total AUM: ~$6.4 billion
Target EBITDA: $15 million to $70 million of annual EBITDA (>$1 million for add-ons)
Rather than relying on financial engineering, Cortec emphasizes operational partnership.
Based in Phoenix, AZ, A1 Garage is a leading provider of residential repair and replacement garage door services across the U.S.
Headquartered in Las Vegas, NV, Goettl is a leading provider of HVAC and plumbing maintenance, repair, and replacement services to the single-family residential market
Little Sleepies is a direct-to-consumer sleepwear and accessories brand
Fund VII exited the majority of its investment in Groundworks in March 2023; concurrent with that transaction, Fund VII and Fund VIII each made a minority investment in the company.
Equity per platform: Up to $500 million of equity committed per platform
Cortec meets regularly with each portfolio company's leadership team to problem-solve collectively
Roughly 90% of its platforms have been entrepreneur-led or family-owned businesses
Platform investments: 43
Add-on acquisitions: 160
Cortec is headquartered at 140 E 45th St, 43rd Floor, New York, NY 10017.
Cortec uses control investment strategy to drive value creation through operational improvements, strategic add-on acquisitions, and organic growth initiatives.
Management teams leverage Cortec to address challenges and capitalize on opportunities.
David L. Schnadig: Co-President
The firm is a differentiated, value-added partner for entrepreneurs, families, and management teams
Platform realizations: 29
In practice, the firm helps prioritize building teams
Since its founding, Palladium has acquired more than 230–240 businesses across 41 platform investments and completed 200+ add-on acquisitions.
Palladium's assets under management are approximately $3.6 billion as of December 31, 2025
Palladium's Flagship strategy makes control-oriented equity investments of $50–150M targeting companies with $10–75M in EBITDA.
Palladium has invested approximately $1.5 billion in the U.S. Hispanic market since 2000, and states that 60% of its portfolio companies are U.S. Hispanic businesses.
Accupac Revenue: $150M–$450M(directional)
Palladium Heritage is a joint venture focused on the lower middle market, targeting B2B and Industrial companies with $3–12M EBITDA and $10–50M of invested capital.
The claimed U.S. Hispanic-market concentration is not visible in the majority of portfolio company descriptions.

Yet across the 18 active portfolio companies profiled in this dossier, only Del Real Foods [del-real-foods-001] and Quirch Foods [quirch-foods-001] are explicitly described as Hispanic-cuisine or Hispanic-market-focused businesses in their own company materials, with Source Logistics [source-logistics-001] a partial third case

Disclosed buy-and-build activity is concentrated in two companies, not spread evenly across the portfolio. Palladium states that its portfolio companies average 4+ add-on acquisitions each [firm-profiler-011]. But of the 18 active companies in this portfolio, only two — Superior Environmental Solutions (6 add-ons since August 2023)

The other 16 companies' zero documented add-on transactions in these materials. Combining the firm's stated 4+ average with this observed concentration suggests either that add-on activity for most portfolio companies goes unannounced relative to SES's and Envoy's heavily press-released acquisition programs, or that the buy-and-build thesis is being executed unevenly across the current portfolio — a pattern the firm's own materials do not surface.

Accupac is a North American-based brand development partner providing formulation, manufacturing, packaging, and innovative solutions for the personal care and beauty end-markets, founded in 1974 and specializing in complex liquid topical and oral care products.

Cobalt Labs, Accupac's R&D arm, partners with sustainability-oriented customers to customize sustainable product formulas around recyclability, virgin-material content, and clean/all-natural designations.
Bodewell Group is a strategic communications firm driving outcomes for public and private sector organizations, with a focus on the infrastructure sector.
Bodewell Group HQ Location: San Diego, CA
Employee Count Estimate: Approximately 50–200 employees-inferred from the company's six-facility scale and Palladium Heritage's stated lower middle-market target size
Daniel's is a tech-enabled jewelry retailer serving a diverse customer base across the continental United States.

On March 20, 2026, Palladium announced an agreement to acquire a majority equity interest in DME Express from WayPoint Capital Partners — Palladium's second hospice-industry investment and second platform investment by Palladium Equity Partners VI, LP.
Fora Financial is a tech-enabled lending platform offering flexible working capital solutions to small and medium-sized businesses via proprietary technology.

Its brand portfolio includes Panamei Seafood®, Mambo Foods®, and exclusive distribution of Chiquita® Frozen tropical fruits, High River Angus®, Kikiriquirch® poultry, and a licensed Certified Angus Beef® agreement.
Kymera is highlighted in Palladium's 2025 Annual Stewardship Report for its 'Safety First - Stop. Think. Act.' safety program
Consumer — Experiential / Out-of-Home Entertainment, aligning with Palladium's stated Flagship theme (Confidence: Medium — sector alignment inferred)

In 2025, the medical technology company overhauled talent acquisition (AI-enabled candidate screening, structured behavioral interviews, defined hiring KPIs) and expanded retention programming, reportedly contributing to a 57% increase in families actively receiving services.
The firm is famous for its exit of the the majority investment of YETI.
Founded June 2008, the company has provided over $2 billion to borrowers to almost all 50 states.
